description_long: This factory building is a large, multi-story structure with a predominantly rectangular footprint and a series of horizontal bands of windows running along its length. The facade features repetitive, industrial-style window arrangements, with the left side of the building showcasing a rounded corner and stacked window bays. The roof is mostly flat, with a prominent vertical structure rising from the center, possibly serving as a service shaft or elevator tower. The exterior is finished in a light gray color, with subtle textural differences indicating concrete or similar materials. The building's design is utilitarian, emphasizing function over ornamentation, and is typical of mid-to-late 20th-century industrial architecture.
description: A large, rectangular, multi-story factory building with horizontal bands of industrial windows and a flat roof, finished in light gray concrete. The structure features a rounded corner and a central vertical shaft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the building, horizontal window bands, and the central vertical shaft.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the building's depth, the rounded corner, and stacked window bays.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side's window arrangement and the extension of the central shaft.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the building's long, flat facade and the layered window sections.
